meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solve Debugging Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your freelance clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging fatigue kills freelance velocity. By adopting a production-grade SaaS starter kit, you shift from writing boilerplate to solving business logic. Pairing this with AI-driven agents like Antigravity and Claude Code automates the identification and resolution of bugs, allowing you to ship resilient, client-ready applications at 3x the speed.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of stack traces at 3 AM. The client wants a feature launch by morning, but your middleware is throwing silent errors that refuse to surface. This is the 'debugging trap'—the silent killer of freelance profitability that turns high-margin projects into bottomless pits of unpaid maintenance hours.

But the best in the business don't write from scratch. They leverage battle-tested SaaS starter kits as their foundation, treating code as a modular asset rather than an artisanal craft. By combining this architecture with autonomous agents, you stop fighting the compiler and start architecting solutions that actually scale.

steps.title: Clone and Initialize the Starter Architecture
steps.description: Select a modular SaaS starter kit that includes pre-configured authentication, database ORM, and error boundaries. This provides the 'ground truth' for your AI agents to reference.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase architecture and identify the primary error handling patterns in the middleware layers. Suggest a unified logging strategy.

steps.title: Automate Traceability with AI Agents
steps.description: Integrate your AI agents to monitor logs in real-time. By setting up structured logging, you allow Claude Code to 'see' state changes before they crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a custom error-interceptor that captures request state and pushes it to an ephemeral debugging buffer for AI analysis.

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases via Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Don't just fix bugs; prevent them. Use your agent to generate comprehensive test suites based on the starter kit's existing patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review existing controller logic and generate 5 edge-case unit tests that cover null-pointers and database timeout scenarios.

steps.title: Refactor Legacy Fragments using AI Context
steps.description: When you encounter 'un-debuggable' code, use the agent to refactor it into the starter kit's standardized functional patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this legacy service fragment to align with the project's dependency injection container. Remove all implicit global state.

steps.title: Deploy with Automated Health-Checks
steps.description: Before handing off to the client, configure your CI/CD pipeline to include AI-driven linting and health verification checks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment-ready health check script that verifies database connectivity, environment variables, and API route availability.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'context file' in your root directory that summarizes your tech stack for Claude Code; it prevents the AI from hallucinating framework versions.
Use Antigravity to create a 'Diff-Map' before running major refactors to visualize exactly which modules are impacted by state changes.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a read-only template; create a 'custom-logic' folder for your client-specific code to keep the core clean.
Configure your AI agent to auto-generate JSDoc comments during every commit; it forces the model to explain its reasoning, making your code easier to maintain later.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'explain the data flow as a sequence diagram'—visualizing the flow often reveals the bug that the text code obscured.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is stabilized, start building a library of reusable 'AI-verified' components that you can drag and drop into every new client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours in the debugger and start shipping high-value features that keep your clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
